title: Lifecycle Hooks
content:
### Lifecycle Diagram

Every Vue component goes through a series of lifecycle stages from creation to destruction:

```
createApp → beforeCreate → created → beforeMount → mounted → beforeUpdate → updated → beforeUnmount → unmounted
```

### beforeCreate

Called after the instance is initialized but before data observation and event setup.

### created

Called after reactive data, computed properties, methods, and watchers are set up. DOM not yet available.

```js
created() {
  console.log('Component created', this.someData)
}
```

### beforeMount

Called right before the component is mounted to the DOM.

### mounted

Called after the component is mounted to the DOM. Access to DOM elements and third-party libraries:

```js
mounted() {
  this.$refs.canvas.getContext('2d')
}
```

### beforeUpdate

Called when reactive data changes, before the DOM is re-rendered.

### updated

Called after the DOM has been re-rendered due to a data change.

### beforeUnmount

Called right before the component is unmounted. Clean up side effects:

```js
beforeUnmount() {
  clearInterval(this.timer)
}
```

### unmounted

Called after the component is fully destroyed.

### Composition API Equivalents

| Options API | Composition API |
|---|---|
| beforeCreate | setup() |
| created | setup() |
| beforeMount | onBeforeMount |
| mounted | onMounted |
| beforeUpdate | onBeforeUpdate |
| updated | onUpdated |
| beforeUnmount | onBeforeUnmount |
| unmounted | onUnmounted |

quiz.question: Which hook is called after the component is rendered to the DOM?
Which Options API hooks map to setup() in Composition API?
